Naga – beesayatv https://beesayatv.com/ Documenting Cebu trails, one quiet hike at a time. Mon, 20 Jul 2026 05:38:49 +0000 en-US hourly 1 https://wordpress.org/?v=7.0.2 https://beesayatv.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/cropped-09-32x32.png Naga – beesayatv https://beesayatv.com/ 32 32 Cogon-Mt. Naupa-Balirong Trail Loop | Cogon / Balirong, Naga | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/cogon-mt-naupa-balirong-trail-loop-cogon-balirong-naga-2026-gpx/ Sat, 18 Jul 2026 23:17:16 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=862 The Cogon–Mt. Naupa–Balirong Trail Loop is an 11 km loop hike in Naga, Cebu. The route combines community trails, rural roads, and open countryside as it passes through the Mt. Naupa area before descending toward Balirong and returning to Cogon. The documented hike took place in July 2026 under dry trail conditions.

Trail Snapshot

MILD
View others →
DocumentedJuly 2026
Distance11 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain421 m
Duration3.5 - 4 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeLimited
Surface30% trail, 70% road
GPXSupporter Access

Access

The documented hike began near Cogon Chapel, where motorcycle parking was available without a parking fee. Road access to the trailhead was via a concrete road. Public transportation was limited during this visit, while habal-habal services and private vehicle access were available.

Why this trail is rated Mild

✓ Mostly clear trail with occasional navigation awareness required
✓ Moderate hiking distance
✓ Sustained but manageable climbs or descents
✓ Some isolated sections between communities
✓ Assistance may require additional time to reach

On this trail: farmland • rolling hills • viewpoint

Route Description

The route follows a combination of rolling community trails, rural roads, and open countryside, with continuous landscape views for much of the hike. Short climbs and descents alternate throughout the loop, creating a steady cardiovascular workout without long sustained ascents or descents. Houses and local activity are present along much of the route, so it rarely feels isolated, although a few quieter sections require additional navigation awareness where the trail becomes faint.

After leaving the Mt. Naupa area, parts of the original route had been cultivated, making it take some time to locate the next trail segment. A descent into the valley toward Balirong also includes a faint trail, suggesting relatively few people use that section. Near the Balirong exit, the route crosses a simple river before continuing toward Cogon Road, which forms the final climb back to Cogon Chapel. This final section is a steady uphill road with very few flat sections before reaching the starting point.

Trail Conditions

The trail was dry during the documented hike, with a cloudy morning providing the day’s weather. Approximately 30% of the route followed trails, while the remaining 70% used roads. Shade was limited, and carrying at least 2 liters of water was recommended.

Mobile signal remained strong throughout most of the hike, with only a few dead spots. Stores were available near communities along the route. Insect activity was high, and the hike encountered mostly local residents, with a few hikers near the Mt. Naupa summit.

Additional Notes

The routes were generally well-trodden and regularly used by both locals and livestock. Although some sections felt isolated, motorcycles from nearby roads could still be heard.

Several narrow concrete road improvement projects were present along the route, although work appeared to be stalled during the documented hike, with no machinery observed. It was mango season, resulting in many fallen and rotting mangoes beneath plantations. Some roadside sections also passed pig farms, making those areas noticeably smelly.

Cows were present in the area but did not block the trail during this hike. Friendly conversations took place with several local residents, including children and adults, who were described as helpful and welcoming.

]]> Alpaco Trail Loop | Alpaco, Naga City | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/alpaco-trail-loop/ Wed, 24 Jun 2026 14:13:01 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=125 Alpaco Trail Loop is a 12 km loop hike in Pinamungahan, Cebu, connecting Veloso Lake, Purok Hoybesan, Bandilaan, and Alpaco. The route follows a combination of rural roads and community trails with straightforward navigation. While the terrain is generally non-technical, the distance and extended exposed sections require moderate physical effort.

Trail Snapshot

MILD
View others →
DocumentedJune 2026
Distance12 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain244 m
Duration3-4 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeModerate
Surface20% trail, 80% road
GPXSupporter Access

Access

The documented hike began near the trail leading to Veloso Lake, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was very limited. Habal-habal services were available.

The documented jump-off provided the quickest access to Veloso Lake but required a short hike, as four-wheel vehicles could not pass the narrow access trail. The other documented approaches to the lake were via Sibago and Alpaco.

Why this trail is rated Mild

✓ Mostly easy-to-follow trail
✓ Occasional people encountered
✓ Longer hiking distance
✓ Long exposed sections with little shade
✓ Limited turnaround points

On this trail: forest • lake • roads • viewpoint

Route Description

The route follows a long but straightforward loop through Veloso Lake, Purok Hoybesan, Bandilaan, and Alpaco. Navigation was easy throughout the hike, with occasional encounters with local residents. Most of the route was non-technical, although the overall distance and long exposed sections made the hike more demanding, particularly during hot weather.

Approximately 80% of the route followed concrete roads with very little shade. Habal-habal vehicles regularly passed through these roads, making it possible to shorten the hike if necessary.

Veloso Lake forms part of the route, although the documented hike treats it as a short detour within the larger loop.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were cloudy, with a mix of dry and muddy trail sections. Most of the route was exposed, with limited shade. Stores were available near several communities along the route.

Mobile signal was strong near the start before becoming intermittent farther along the route. Between 1 and 2 liters of water was recommended. Few people and minimal insect activity were observed during the hike.

Additional Notes

Parts of the route passed through pig-raising areas where strong livestock odors were observed.

Several dogs were encountered, particularly near homes and communities. Carrying a hiking stick and giving dogs adequate space was recommended during the documented hike.

The route passes through several communities where the local residents encountered during the hike were observed to be friendly and approachable.
